title: $prompt
text: $prompt Return or set the position for the cursor in the Form when the current ProcScript module ends. $prompt $prompt = Field Return Values String that contains the name of the field where the cursor will be positioned when control returns to the structure editor. The returned value is in the format FIELD.ENTITY.MODEL . Empty string ( "" ) if the default prompting sequence is in effect. Use Use in Form components. If used in other components, it is ignored. Note: $prompt should not be used in I/O-related triggers that set the active path, such as Retrieve , because $prompt can change the active path. As a result, the sequence of fired triggers will be different from the expected sequence, and some triggers will not fire at all, for example loseFocus and getFocus . Description The function $prompt can be used to define the field where the cursor should be positioned when the current ProcScript module ends. When the name of a field is assigned to $prompt , the cursor is positioned at that field when control returns to the structure editor. When executed, Uniface automatically scrolls to the field where the cursor is positioned. If the field has an associated label, Uniface scrolls to show the entire field including the associated label. This is especially useful in mobile applications in which some fields may not be immediately visible. You can disable this automatic scrolling using the $PROMPT_SCROLL assignment setting. Example: Using $prompt The next trigger ProcScript below defines conditional field prompting. If the invoice date is greater than the system date, the cursor goes automatically to the CORRESP entity. Otherwise, the cursor goes to the next field in the default prompting sequence. trigger next if (INVDATE > $date) $prompt = TEXT.CORRESP message "Invoice is overdue. Write a reminder letter." endif end; next Copy Related Links Active Path for Runtime Processing The active path determines where processing is applied in a runtime component, and the order in which it occurs. It leads from the current occurrence of the outermost entity down to the current occurrence of the innermost entity. $PROMPT_SCROLL Enable or disable automatic scrolling for the $prompt ProcScript function.
